bibl.title: Rāmāṣṭaprāsastava
Aṣṭaprāsarāmaśataka
Aṣṭaprāsa
bibl.note: One of the 9 hymns that are printed in the Stavamaṇimālā as Sarasvatī Vilāsa Series 6 (ed. Subrahmanya Sastri)
See Thiruvengadathan pg. 17 and Raghavan introduction to Śāhendravilāsa pg. 41

In this hymn, Rāma is described as discharging the duties of Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Śiva. Incidents of his life are mentioned. The hymn is called aṣṭaprāsa due to the repetition of a set of letters at intervals throughout the text. The hymn is composed in Śārdūlavikṛīdita metre. (see Sinhā pg. 303)
